decision_envelope.target_outcome: Git and non-Git filesystem roots support parallel, replayable, isolated tasks without silent authority loss during ordinary worktree/root lifecycle.
decision_envelope.decision_flip_condition: If provider routing is not unique cross-platform or HOME cannot serve as the local filesystem control root, require an explicit external authority-dir or shrink non-Git support.

depth_basis: The plan changes critical authority storage, hard-cut behavior, HOME data separation, Git commit certification, and host-hook routing across Git and non-Git roots.

second_model_availability.basis: The collaboration runtime exposes the distinct gpt-5.6-terra model override for a fresh-context read-only subagent.

resolved_budget.basis: The candidate freezes review_budget at four reviewer invocations.

findings.claim: The locator design cannot yet enforce its claimed move-versus-copy and crash guarantees because it defines only a copied authority-id/nonce locator plus a last-observed path, not an atomic identity/attachment state machine that distinguishes a moved root/worktree from two live copies and resolves staged-shard recovery.
findings.evidence: The candidate gives locators only authority/worktree ID, nonce digest, and format generation, while AuthorityState stores only nonce digest and last observed path. It claims both automatic move acceptance and copied-locator collision without defining compare-and-claim, old-path liveness, collision journal, lease epoch, or recovery transitions.
findings.affected_section: AuthorityState 模型; Worktree 身份与生命周期; Filesystem Root 身份与生命周期; Artifact、Criterion 与 Target-Authority 路由; 下一步验证
findings.parent_evidence_and_reason: Confirmed against candidate lines 212-295: the frozen revision stored only nonce/last-observed path and promised automatic move acceptance plus copy collision and staged recovery without claim, anchor, collision, or recovery transitions.
findings.remediation_summary: Added an authority-led staged/claimed/pending/reattach-required/collision/retired state machine, staged and claimed locator digest binding, stable anchor rules, explicit recovery, and fail-open evidence behavior.

findings.claim: Target routing lacks the required explicit exclusion of Git administrative/common-dir and Workloop control paths from ordinary Git-worktree authority selection, permitting protected control writes to be attributed as task evidence.
findings.evidence: The candidate routes every target contained by a Git worktree to the Git authority without a prior git-internal/control-root branch. Current supervision separately protects worktree .git, absolute git-dir, git-common-dir, repository .workloop, and HOME .workloop control roots.
findings.affected_section: Authority Provider 与 Root 选择; Artifact、Criterion 与 Target-Authority 路由; 预计修改范围; 验收预言机
findings.parent_evidence_and_reason: Confirmed against candidate lines 200-206 and 327-343 and the then-current target-routing implementation: the frozen plan selected Git containment before any control-plane branch while current behavior separately recognizes repository .workloop, .git, git-dir, git-common-dir, and HOME .workloop.
findings.remediation_summary: Moved control-plane classification ahead of provider selection, enumerated protected roots, separated internal transactions and host Git receipts, and made direct targets ineligible for task evidence without changing default fail-open hooks.

findings.claim: Whether the selected common-dir and detached-HOME control roots can meet the required durable-lock, atomic-publication, and recovery semantics on every supported platform remains undecided.
findings.evidence: The candidate itself marks common-dir support as unverified and defers macOS/Linux/Windows lock, crash, locator, remove/prune, and torn-tail checks to a future spike; only local remove/prune directory survival is currently verified.
findings.missing_check: Run the frozen spike on macOS, Linux, and the supported Windows matrix: concurrent common-ledger and detached-ledger genesis/append; lock-owner crash before and after append; torn-tail recovery; locator publish crash; linked-worktree remove/prune/move/reuse; assert no duplicate sequence, split authority, or unsafe recovery; choose the fallback design if a guarantee fails.
findings.affected_section: 下一步验证; 真实约束、约定与假设; 目标架构; 范围与成本
findings.gap_scope_reason: The provider boundary is the selected core mechanism, and implementation is explicitly contingent on unverified platform capability.
findings.parent_evidence_and_reason: Confirmed against candidate lines 46-59 and 114-116 plus the test inventory: the candidate explicitly defers the new common-dir/detached-HOME lock, locator publication, and crash matrix; existing Windows coverage exercises the old repository-local authority and cannot prove the new physical layout.
findings.next_check: Run the frozen macOS/Linux/Windows mechanism spike before technical GO or product implementation, then submit its evidence with the exact candidate to the same required reviewer.

findings.claim: The plan needs one explicit lock-order and reentrancy contract spanning attachment claim/routing, authority append, per-worktree Git stage/commit, criterion leases, and best-effort outcome projection.
findings.evidence: Each routing operation validates the locator under the authority lock (Attachment claim 状态机), registration commits claim and task events under that lock, and partitioned Git work adds a separate worktree lock (Git Stage / Commit Receipt). The plan does not state which lock is acquired first, whether routing and append share one non-reentrant transaction, or that criterion/outcome work may never wait while holding an authority or Git lock. Current task-store relies on exactly this serialization distinction for correctness.
findings.affected_section: 目标架构; Attachment claim 状态机; Artifact、Criterion 与 Target-Authority 路由; Git Stage / Commit Receipt; HOME Authority / Projection 隔离与硬切换
findings.parent_evidence_and_reason: Confirmed against candidate lines 269-280, 361-414, and 416-434: the plan names authority, per-worktree Git, criterion lease, and outcome locks but only says criterion runs outside the authority lock; it does not freeze a total order, reentrancy rule, or no-I/O-under-lock boundary. Current task-store/application explicitly separate short authority serialization from external criterion work.
findings.remediation_summary: Added a non-reentrant lock contract with the only legal nesting G->A or C->A, one-authority-at-a-time processing, no O nesting, bounded work inside A, and explicit hook/CLI timeout semantics.

findings.claim: The required recover-attachment, reattach, fork-identity, and cleanup operations need a frozen CLI/provenance contract so failed claim publication and identity collisions have a safe, testable operator path.
findings.evidence: The revised state machine correctly requires explicit recovery after staged/pending crashes and explicit user choice for reattach/fork, but the CLI section does not define these verbs, their authority/attachment/token/path inputs, user-grant requirement, collision preconditions, or resulting state transitions. These are the only permitted exits from several non-routable states.
findings.affected_section: Attachment claim 状态机; Worktree 身份与生命周期; Filesystem Root 身份与生命周期; CLI 语义; 验收预言机
findings.parent_evidence_and_reason: Confirmed against candidate lines 269-280, 294-333, and 458-482: recover-attachment, cleanup, reattach, and fork-identity are the only named exits from non-routable states, but none appears in the CLI inventory and no provenance/precondition/postcondition contract is defined.
findings.remediation_summary: Defined recovery, staged cleanup/abandon, reattach, and fork CLI selectors, provenance, optimistic discriminators, pending/publish/final events, crash idempotence, receipts, and forbidden Hook automation.

round_receipts.measurement_source: caller prompt/result character estimate and local wall-clock snapshots

finding_payloads.claim: Whether the selected common-dir and detached-HOME control roots provide the required durable-lock, atomic-publication, stable-anchor, and recovery semantics on every supported platform remains undecided.
finding_payloads.evidence: The candidate continues to mark common-dir and HOME detached-shard feasibility as unverified assumptions and now explicitly makes the macOS/Linux/Windows mechanism spike an implementation-entry gate. Only local remove/prune survival is currently evidenced; existing Windows tests do not exercise the proposed Git-common authority or locator-anchor layout.
finding_payloads.missing_check: Run the frozen spike on macOS, Linux, and the supported Windows matrix: concurrent common/detached genesis and append; lock owner/reaper and crash boundaries; torn-tail recovery; staged/claimed locator publication; stable-object-ID and cross-volume move behavior; remove/prune/move/reuse; control-plane exclusion. Assert no duplicate sequence, split authority, unsafe recovery, or clean evidence from collision/pending/control-plane targets; select the fallback design if any guarantee fails.
finding_payloads.affected_section: 下一步验证; 真实约束、约定与假设; 目标架构; Attachment claim 状态机; 范围与成本
finding_payloads.gap_scope_reason: The selected provider and anchor mechanisms are explicitly conditional on this cross-platform evidence, so technical GO and implementation remain blocked until it passes.

review_outcome.reason: The exact current revision has not received a complete GO, the decision-blocking cross-platform authority/anchor spike is still missing, and another review invocation cannot close that evidence gap.
review_outcome.exact_next_action: Run the frozen macOS/Linux/Windows mechanism spike in the candidate, attach its evidence, then resume the same required second-model reviewer with a complete review of revision 5ce1a74b9fbd48e8b2714df84ff0a3711c5ef0afa9d451f432b2aa752d0c15d6 or its evidence-only successor.
